dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - May 01, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Diamonds Shattered: Thrilling Comebacks and Dominant Pitching Displayed Across MLB**

In a day filled with electrifying performances, the Chicago Cubs outlasted the Arizona Diamondbacks 6-5, thanks in part to Geraldo Perdomo's second home run of the game. Perdomo's 3-RBI effort propelled the Cubs to victory, while Colin Rea delivered a strong outing, allowing just 2 earned runs over 5.1 innings. In another game, the Philadelphia Phillies edged the Miami Marlins 6-5, with Zack Wheeler tossing 6 scoreless innings and striking out 8.

On the mound, Will Warren was nearly unhittable for the New York Yankees, fanning 9 in 6.1 innings as they cruised past the Baltimore Orioles 7-2. The Houston Astros fell to the Boston Red Sox 3-1, with Jarren Duran hitting his second home run of the game. In the American League West, the Toronto Blue Jays defeated the Minnesota Twins 7-3, behind Kazuma Okamoto's 2-home run, 3-RBI performance.

all_games.top_pitching_line: Colin Rea: 5.1 IP, 6 K, 2 ER, 0 BB, 8 H
all_games.top_batting_line: Geraldo Perdomo: 2 H, 1 HR, 3 RBI, 5 AB
all_games.summary: The Chicago Cubs edged the Arizona Diamondbacks 6-5 behind Colin Rea's 5.1 innings of work and Geraldo Perdomo's 2-hit, 3-RBI performance.
